phase1.output: (Sound effects play when events occur: coins, jumps, attacks, hits, deaths, etc.)
phase1.explanation:
🎮 **Playing SFX on Events connects sound to gameplay.**

**Event-to-sound mapping:**

| Event | Sound |
|-------|-------|
| Collect coin | Coin sound |
| Jump | Jump sound |
| Attack | Attack sound |
| Take damage | Hit sound |
| Die | Death sound |
| Win | Win sound |
| Button click | Click sound |

**SFX Manager benefits:**
- Centralized audio control
- Consistent volume management
- Easy to add new sounds
- Can be accessed from anywhere

**Sharp-chan says:**
"Sound effects make your game feel responsive. Play them on every action."
phase1.openingDialogue: SFX on Events: sound makes actions satisfying. 🔔

phase2.errorMessage:
NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object
SFXManager.Instance is null because the SFXManager wasn't created.

phase3.prompt:
What is the benefit of using a centralized SFX Manager?

A) It makes sounds louder  
B) It centralizes audio control and makes changes easier  
C) It automatically adds reverb  
D) It makes sounds play faster

Select the CORRECT answer.
dialogueHints: Think about why you'd use a manager.
phase3.openingDialogue: SFX check! Why use a manager? 🤔
